output.final_deliverable:
## Fact-check verdicts (C1–C4)

### C1 [Physics]
**Claim:** “The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 metres per second.”

- **Supporting evidence found (but NOT visited):** A NIST CODATA entry exists for the “speed of light in vacuum,” but the attempted visit **timed out**, and no page text was captured.
  - The system’s log shows a candidate authoritative URL: https://physics.nist.gov/cgi-bin/cuu/Value?c= (NIST CODATA) (from MERGED RESULTS / RETRIEVED MEMORIES), but **RAW VISIT CONTENT is empty** and the visit failed.
- **Contradicting evidence found:** None in the provided data.
- **Authoritative source visit requirement:** **Not satisfied** for C1 because the only attempted visit (NIST) failed and there is no RAW VISIT CONTENT.

**Verdict:** **PARTIALLY TRUE** (value is widely known and consistent with the intended definition, but the workflow evidence here is incomplete—no authoritative page content was successfully visited/captured).

**Confidence:** **0.55**

**Justification (URLs used):** NIST page attempt timed out (no extractable quote available): https://physics.nist.gov/cgi-bin/cuu/Value?c=

---

### C2 [Art History]
**Claim:** “Leonardo da Vinci painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel.”

- **Evidence search (supporting vs contradicting):** The search results strongly indicate the ceiling was painted by **Michelangelo**, not Leonardo.
- **Authoritative source visit requirement:** **Not satisfied** in the provided dataset because no URLs were actually visited and **RAW VISIT CONTENT is empty**.
- **Contradicting evidence found (via search results):**
  - Wikipedia result explicitly identifies the work as “Sistine Chapel ceiling” and implies Michelangelo’s role: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sistine_Chapel_ceiling
  - Other reputable art-education sources returned (Smarthistory, Khan Academy), also about Michelangelo’s ceiling: https://smarthistory.org/michelangelo-ceiling-of-the-sistine-chapel/ and https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/ap-art-history/early-europe-and-colonial-americas/renaissance-art-europe-ap/a/michelangelo-ceiling-of-the-sistine-chapel

**Verdict:** **FALSE** (the ceiling is attributed to Michelangelo, not Leonardo da Vinci).

**Confidence:** **0.90**

**Justification (URLs used):** Search results consistently point to Michelangelo as the ceiling’s painter, not Leonardo: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sistine_Chapel_ceiling ; https://smarthistory.org/michelangelo-ceiling-of-the-sistine-chapel/

---

### C3 [Biology]
**Claim:** “Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.”

- **Evidence search:** Multiple sources in the search results explicitly state both “three hearts” and “blue blood.”
- **Authoritative source visit requirement:** **Not satisfied** in the provided dataset because no URLs were visited and **RAW VISIT CONTENT is empty**.
- **Supporting evidence found (via search results):**
  - Natural History Museum (UK) result states: “An octopus has blue blood and three hearts.” https://www.nhm.ac.uk/discover/octopuses-keep-surprising-us-here-are-eight-examples-how.html
  - Smithsonian Magazine also returned with the same combined fact in the title/description: https://www.smithsonianmag.com/science-nature/ten-wild-facts-about-octopuses-they-have-three-hearts-big-brains-and-blue-blood-7625828/
- **Contradicting evidence found:** None in the provided data.

**Verdict:** **TRUE** (as supported by multiple reputable sources in the provided search results).

**Confidence:** **0.85**

**Justification (URLs used):** Natural History Museum and Smithsonian both support “three hearts” and “blue blood”: https://www.nhm.ac.uk/discover/octopuses-keep-surprising-us-here-are-eight-examples-how.html ; https://www.smithsonianmag.com/science-nature/ten-wild-facts-about-octopuses-they-have-three-hearts-big-brains-and-blue-blood-7625828/

---

### C4 [Geography]
**Claim:** “Mount Everest is located on the border between Nepal and India.”

- **Evidence search:** Multiple authoritative sources (Wikipedia, Britannica) state Everest lies on the **Nepal–China (Tibet Autonomous Region)** border, not Nepal–India.
- **Authoritative source visit requirement:** **Not satisfied** in the provided dataset because no URLs were visited and **RAW VISIT CONTENT is empty**.
- **Contradicting evidence found (via search results):**
  - Wikipedia snippet: Everest “marks part of the China–Nepal border at its summit.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mount_Everest
  - Britannica snippet: “It lies on the border between Nepal and the Tibet Autonomous Region of China.” https://www.britannica.com/place/Mount-Everest
- **Supporting evidence found:** None in the provided data (no credible source returned claiming Nepal–India border).

**Verdict:** **FALSE**

**Confidence:** **0.95**

**Justification (URLs used):** Authoritative references place Everest on the Nepal–China/Tibet border: https://www.britannica.com/place/Mount-Everest ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mount_Everest

---

## VERDICT REPORT

### Verdict table

| Claim ID | Verdict | Confidence | Key Evidence | Source URL |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| C1 | PARTIALLY TRUE | 0.55 | Intended authoritative source (NIST CODATA) could not be visited; claim matches the standard defined value but is not proven with captured authoritative page text in this run. | https://physics.nist.gov/cgi-bin/cuu/Value?c= |
| C2 | FALSE | 0.90 | Search results identify the Sistine Chapel ceiling with Michelangelo (not Leonardo da Vinci). | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sistine_Chapel_ceiling ; https://smarthistory.org/michelangelo-ceiling-of-the-sistine-chapel/ |
| C3 | TRUE | 0.85 | Reputable sources (Natural History Museum, Smithsonian) state octopuses have “three hearts” and “blue blood.” | https://www.nhm.ac.uk/discover/octopuses-keep-surprising-us-here-are-eight-examples-how.html ; https://www.smithsonianmag.com/science-nature/ten-wild-facts-about-octopuses-they-have-three-hearts-big-brains-and-blue-blood-7625828/ |
| C4 | FALSE | 0.95 | Wikipedia and Britannica place Everest on the Nepal–China/Tibet border, not Nepal–India. | https://www.britannica.com/place/Mount-Everest ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mount_Everest |

### Reliability summary (100+ words)
C2, C3, and C4 were straightforward to verify at the **search stage** because multiple reputable and/or authoritative references consistently agreed on the core facts: Michelangelo painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling (not Leonardo), octopuses are widely described as having three hearts and blue blood, and Mount Everest is repeatedly described as lying on the Nepal–China/Tibet border rather than Nepal–India. C4 was especially clear because both Wikipedia and Britannica converge on the same border description. C1 should have been the easiest (it’s a defined SI value), but it required deeper investigation in this specific run because the attempted authoritative source visit to NIST timed out and no RAW VISIT CONTENT was captured, preventing the required direct quotation-based verification. Overall, the main reliability limitation here is the absence of successful page visits and verbatim extracts from authoritative pages in the provided dataset.

## Evidence limitations (important)
- **RAW VISIT CONTENT is empty for all URLs**, so I cannot provide the required verbatim quotes from visited pages.
- The only recorded visit attempt (NIST for C1) failed with a timeout: https://physics.nist.gov/cgi-bin/cuu/Value?c=
- For C2–C4, only search results are available; no authoritative page was visited/captured in the provided execution data.
output.action_summary: Verdicts: C1 PARTIALLY TRUE (insufficient captured authoritative evidence due to NIST timeout), C2 FALSE, C3 TRUE, C4 FALSE. Confidence highest for C4 and C2 due to consistent authoritative search results; verification quality is limited overall because no RAW VISIT CONTENT was captured and visit requirements were not met for C2–C4.

results.title: Sistine Chapel ceiling - Wikipedia
results.description: The ceiling was painted at the commission of Pope Julius II. The ceiling's various painted elements form part of a larger scheme of decoration within the chapel. Prior to Michelangelo's contribution, the walls were painted by several leading artists of the late 15th century including Sandro ...

results.title: Smarthistory – Michelangelo, Ceiling of the Sistine Chapel
results.description: Steven Zucker: [0:04] We’re in the Sistine Chapel in the Vatican, which has tremendous importance to Catholicism. This is were the pope will lead mass, but perhaps most famously, this is the room that the College of Cardinals uses to decide the next pope. Dr. Beth Harris: [0:19] Every surface of this space is decorated, from the beautiful mosaics on the floor, the walls are painted with frescoes by early Renaissance artists, the wall behind the altar was painted by Michelangelo later in his life, and then of course, the ceiling.

results.title: Michelangelo’s Painting of the Sistine Chapel Ceiling
results.description: The walls were adorned with frescoes by different artists, such as Pietro Perugino, who painted Christ delivering the keys to St. Peter there in 1482. In 1508, Pope Julius II (reigned 1503-1513) hired Michelangelo to paint the ceiling of the chapel, rather than leaving it appear as it had.

results.title: Michelangelo frescoed the ceiling and the Last Judgment
results.description: It was Pope Julius II who chose Michelangelo Buonarroti to decorate the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel. Michelangelo completed the ceiling and vault decoration in just 4 years. Initially, he was only supposed to paint the figures of the 12 Apostles, ...

results.title: Mount Everest - Wikipedia
results.description: Mount Everest (known locally as Sagarmāthā in Nepal and Qomolangma in Tibet) is Earth's highest mountain above sea level. It lies in the Mahalangur Himal sub-range of the Himalayas and marks part of the China–Nepal border at its summit. Its height was most recently measured in 2020 by Chinese ...

results.title: Mount Everest | Height, Map, Deaths, Facts, & Climbers | Britannica
results.description: Reinhold Messner completed the first solo ascent of Mount Everest in 1980. Mount Everest is on the crest of the Great Himalayas of southern Asia. It lies on the border between Nepal and the Tibet Autonomous Region of China.

results.title: r/geography on Reddit: Is the highest peak of mountain Everest equally split by the borders of Nepal and china, or does one actually own it?
results.description: It's shared but it's generally ... China have their highest peaks cut in half by the Chinese border. ... Nepal shares Everest with Tibet, not China....

results.title: Where is Mount Everest? Location, Country & Height Facts
results.description: Everest Geographic Location: Mount Everest is located on the border between Nepal and the Tibet Autonomous Region of China, in the Mahalangur Himal sub-range of the Himalayas. Mount Everest is not located in India, and it is the truth.

results.title: Where is Mount Everest — Is it in China or Nepal?
results.description: Known as the world's highest mountain, with a recent measurement of 8,848.86 meters (29,031.34), Mount Everest is part of the Himalayas Mountain range on the border of Nepal and China.

details.summary_quality: High. The reliability summary is detailed, exceeds 100 words, and clearly explains which claims were easy to verify (C2, C3, C4) and why C1 had an evidence-collection issue. It also transparently notes the absence of raw visit content and the NIST timeout. The main weakness is the incorrect labeling of C1 as PARTIALLY TRUE despite the well-established defined SI value; otherwise the report is thorough and properly cites authoritative URLs.
